'''Bureta Faraimo''' (born 16 July 1990) is a [[United States national rugby league team|United States international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays on the {{rlp|wg}} for [[Hunslet RLFC|Hunslet]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan from [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]].

He has previously played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[NRL]], and [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]].

==Early life==
Faraimo was born in [[Wellington]], New Zealand, and is of [[Tokelau]]n descent (Olohega) also known as [[Swains Island]] which is a part of [[American Samoa]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/mackay-cutters-winger-bureta-faraimo-on-cusp-of-realising-unlikely-nrl-dream/news-story/fc5be64ea6779866850921f4d1df8650|title=Mackay Cutters winger Bureta Faraimo on cusp of realising unlikely NRL dream|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|date=2013-09-18|access-date=2016-12-18}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for St. George, New Zealand.

==Club career==
===New Zealand===
In 2010, Faraimo trialed with the [[New Zealand Warriors]] Under 20s team but didn't get a contract.<ref name="stuff1">[http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/4121374/A-stroll-in-the-park-for-Faraimo-and-the-Orcas A stroll in the park for Faraimo and the Orcas] ''stuff.co.nz'', 13 August 2010</ref> Later that year, while playing for the [[Wainuiomata Lions]], Faraimo was selected to play for the [[Wellington rugby league team|Wellington Orcas]] in the [[NZRL Men's National Competition|National Competition]], scoring 12 tries in his first 3 games.

===Mackay Cutters===
In 2011, Faraimo moved to Australia to play for the [[Mackay Cutters]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] for more [[rugby league]] exposure. In just half a season, Faraimo had scored 29 tries.<ref>[http://www.dailymercury.com.au/news/three-reasons-to-fear-faraimo/1872467/ Three reasons to fear Faraimo] ''dailymercury.com.au'', 18 May 2013</ref>

===Parramatta Eels===
In November 2013, due to the exposure from the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]], Faraimo signed a two-year contract with [[National Rugby League]] team, the [[Parramatta Eels]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailymercury.com.au/news/bureta-faraimo-joining-parramatta-eels/2091674/|title=Bureta Faraimo is joining up with the Parramatta Eels | Mackay Daily Mercury|publisher=Dailymercury.com.au|access-date=2016-03-18}}</ref>

In round 21 of the [[2014 NRL season]], Faraimo made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for Parramatta against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]].<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2014/08/02/nrl_late_mail_round_21.html|title=NRL Late Mail: Round 21 - Eels|date=2 August 2014 |publisher=Parraeels.com.au|access-date=2016-03-18}}</ref> He scored two tries on debut in the club's 32–12 win at [[Remondis Stadium]].

On 21 September 2014, Faraimo was named at {{rlp|wg}} in the 2014 [[New South Wales Cup]] Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2014/09/21/_2014_vb_nsw_cup_team_of_the_year.html|title=2014 VB NSW Cup Team of the Year|publisher=NSWRL|access-date=2016-03-18|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150708221216/http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2014/09/21/_2014_vb_nsw_cup_team_of_the_year.html|archive-date=8 July 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref>

On 4 April 2015, Faraimo re-signed with Parramatta on a one-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2015/04/04/eels_re_signing_news.html|title=Eels Re-Signing News - Eels|date=4 April 2015 |publisher=Parraeels.com.au|access-date=2016-03-18}}</ref>

On 3 May 2016, Faraimo played for the [[New South Wales Residents rugby league team|New South Wales Residents]] against the [[Queensland Residents rugby league team|Queensland Residents]].<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL Digital Media |url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2016/05/01/intrust_super_premie.html |title=UPDATED: 2016 ISP NSW Residents Team |date=3 May 2016 |publisher=NSWRL |access-date=2017-07-21}}</ref>

On 2 May 2017, Faraimo played for the New South Wales Residents against the Queensland Residents for the second year in a row.<ref>{{cite web |author=NRL Digital Media |url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2017/05/02/_2017_intrust_super_.html |title=UPDATED SQUAD | Intrust Super Premiership NSW Residents |publisher=NSWRL |access-date=2017-07-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170807021601/http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2017/05/02/_2017_intrust_super_.html |archive-date=7 August 2017 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

===New Zealand Warriors===
In July 2017, Faraimo joined the [[New Zealand Warriors]] for the remainder of the season, after being released from his Eels contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/parramatta-eels-release-bureta-faraimo-28098/ |title=Parramatta Eels release Bureta Faraimo |date=3 July 2017 |publisher=Zero Tackle |access-date=2017-07-03}}</ref> Although joining the Warriors for the remainder of 2017, he planned to join the [[Super League]] in 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2017/07/03/outside_back_signed_.html|title=Outside back signed as injury cover|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|date=3 July 2017 |access-date=3 July 2017}}</ref> On 28 August 2017, he was named on the wing in the 2017 [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2017/08/28/_2017_team_of_the_ye_1478769739.html|title=2017 Team Of The Year - ISP NSW|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|date=28 August 2017 |access-date=31 August 2017}}</ref> He didn't play an NRL game for the Warriors.

===Hull F.C.===
In August 2017, he signed a two-year contract with Super League side [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], starting in 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/hull-fc-sign-nrl-duo-2018-28501/|title=Hull FC sign NRL duo for 2018 - Zero Tackle|date=1 August 2017|access-date=14 September 2017}}</ref>

He played 13 games for [[Hull F.C.]] in the 2020 Super League season including the club's semi-final defeat against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] as they got within one game of the grand final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-hull-fc-highlights-4716172#964303/|title=Wigan Warriors v Hull FC highlights as FC struggle to find play-off form in one-sided defeat|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|date=19 November 2020 }}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
In round 18 of the 2022 Super League season, Faraimo scored two tries in Castleford's 34-20 loss against Leeds at [[Magic Weekend]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12196/12648675/super-league-magic-weekend-day-1-at-newcastles-st-james-park-live/|title=Saints, Toulouse and Leeds all win on Magic Weekend|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
In round 20 of the 2022 Super League season, Faraimo scored two tries for Castleford in a 46-18 victory over [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12657086/super-league-derrell-olpherts-stars-as-ruthless-castleford-tigers-stun-hull-fc-46-18/|title=Super League: Derrell Olpherts stars as ruthless Castleford Tigers stun Hull FC 46-18|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
In round 22 of the 2022 Super League season, Faraimo scored a [[hat-trick]] in Castleford's 20-12 loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/aug/07/st-helens-castleford-super-league-match-report/|title=Nervy St Helens overcome Castleford thanks to boot of Tommy Makinson|website=www.theguardian.com|date=7 August 2022 }}</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC===
On 4 August 2023, it was announced that he, along with [[Mahe Fonua]] would join [[Doncaster RLFC]] for the remainder of the 2023 season, following the pair's release from Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-released-overseas-duo-join-league-1-outfit-for-remainder-of-season|title=Castleford Tigers: Released overseas duo join League 1 outfit for remainder of season|website=Love Rugby League|date=4 Aug 2023}}</ref>
Faraimo only played one game for Doncaster, the League One play-off grand final victory over the [[North Wales Crusaders]]. In February 2024, Faraimo announced he would not be playing for Doncaster in 2024.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-and-hull-fc-winger-bureta-faraimo-interested-in-super-league-move-following-doncaster-exit/|title=Ex-Castleford Tigers and Hull FC winger Bureta Faraimo interested in Super League move following Doncaster exit|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

On 3 May 2024 it was announced that [[Doncaster RLFC]] had re-signed Faraimo for the remainder of the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=3 May 2024|title=Bureta Faraimo: Former Castleford Tigers and Hull FC winger makes Doncaster return|url=https://www.msn.com/en-gb/sport/rugbyleague/bureta-faraimo-former-castleford-tigers-and-hull-fc-winger-makes-doncaster-return/ar-AA1o5BnT?ocid=msedgdhp&pc=U531&cvid=728255a44e10483bcb80be42ec393916&ei=28}}</ref>

===Hunslet RLFC (loan)===
On 20 February 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hunslet RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Feb 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Hull FC ace among duo to make cross-Championship loan switch|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bureta-faraimo-joins-hunslet-in-loan-switch}}</ref>

==Representative career==
In 2009 and 2010, Faraimo played for the [[New Zealand Residents]].<ref>[http://www.nzrl.co.nz/nz-residents/nz-residents-2010.aspx NZ Residents 2010] ''nzrl.co.nz''</ref>

In 2011, Faraimo played for the Queensland Rangers team.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.leagueunlimited.com/news/show/queensland-rangers-team/ |title=Queensland Rangers Team » League Unlimited |access-date=2013-11-21 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20131121150119/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/news/show/queensland-rangers-team/ |archive-date=2013-11-21 }}</ref>

In 2013, Faraimo was selected in the [[United States national rugby league team|United States]] [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] squad, qualifying because his father was born on [[Swains Island]], an island administered by the [[United States]] as part of [[American Samoa]].<ref>{{usurped|[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304042601/http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1148/-man-squads-for-all-rlwc 24-man squads for all RLWC2013 nations announced]}} ''rlwc2013.com'', 9 October 2013</ref><ref>[http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/mackay-cutters-winger-bureta-faraimo-on-cusp-of-realising-unlikely-nrl-dream/story-fni3fbgz-1226721339087 Mackay Cutters winger Bureta Faraimo on cusp of realising unlikely NRL dream] ''dailytelegraph.com.au'', 18 September 2013</ref>

Faraimo competed in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]] for the United States, featuring in all three pool matches: losing 12–58 to [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]], 0–46 to [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]] and 0–64 to [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/bureta-faraimos-world-cup-stats-744713|title=Why Faraimo's World Cup stats show he's right fit for Hull FC|first=Paul|last=Clarke|date=8 November 2017|access-date=23 November 2017}}</ref>

Faraimo returned to action for the United States two years later, playing in the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]] before heading to [[Jacksonville]], Florida for the nation's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] qualifier match against [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]].<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/hull-fc-winger-bureta-faraimo-3565210| title = Bureta Faraimo's crazy month won't derail his Hull FC chance as winger returns to training - Hull Live| date = 22 November 2019}}</ref>
